back_translation: Now the apostles and the brothers who were throughout the province of Judea heard that the Gentiles also had welcomed-received the word of God.
When Peter went up to the city of Jerusalem, the believers who were circumcised disputed with him.
"You entered the house of people who have not been circumcised and ate food together with them."
So Peter explained the whole matter in order for them to hear, saying:

"While I was praying in the city of Joppa, I saw a vision in a trance — there was a kind of container like a large linen sheet being let down from heaven, tied at its four corners, and it descended down right up to me.
When I fixed my gaze on that container, I saw the four-footed animals of the earth, wild beasts, reptiles, and birds of the air.
I also heard a voice speaking to me, 'Peter, arise, kill, and eat.'
But I replied to the Lord, 'O Lord, this is utterly impossible, because a thing that is defiled or impure has never entered into my mouth.'
But the voice from heaven answered a second time, 'What God has cleansed, you must not call defiled.'
This event happened three times, and then everything was taken back up into heaven again.
At that very moment, three men who had been sent from the city of Caesarea to me arrived at the house where we were staying.
The Spirit told me to go with them without hesitating in heart, and these six brothers also went with me; we then entered the house of that man.
He told us that he had seen an angel standing in his house and saying, 'Send people to the city of Joppa to fetch Simon who has the nickname Peter.
Peter will speak words to you, which are words that will cause you and everyone in your household to receive salvation.'
When I began to speak, the Holy Spirit descended upon them just as he had descended upon us at the very beginning.
Then I remembered the word of the Lord that he spoke, 'John baptized with water, but you will receive baptism with the Holy Spirit.'
Therefore, given that God granted the same gift to them as he granted to us who believed in the Lord Jesus Christ, who indeed am I that I could hinder God?"
When they heard these things, they fell silent and offered honor to God, saying, 'So then, God has granted repentance leading to life to the Gentiles too.'

As for those who were scattered due to the tribulation from the persecution that arose on account of Stephen, they traveled as far as the province of Phoenicia, the island of Cyprus, and the city of Antioch; they proclaimed the word only to Jewish people alone.

But there were some among them, namely Cypriot and Cyrenian men, who when they arrived at the city of Antioch also proclaimed to the Greeks [that is, Gentiles who spoke Greek], proclaiming the good news concerning the Lord Jesus.
The hand of the Lord was present with them, and a great number of people believed and turned back to the Lord.
The news concerning those people reached the church in the city of Jerusalem; they therefore sent Barnabas to go as far as the city of Antioch.
When Barnabas arrived and saw the grace of God, he rejoiced and encouraged everyone to hold fast to the Lord with a focused heart.
For Barnabas was a good man, full-overflowing with the Holy Spirit and with faith, and a great number of people were brought to be disciples of the Lord.
Then Barnabas departed and traveled to the city of Tarsus in order to seek out Saul.

When he had found Saul, he brought him to the city of Antioch. The two of them met together with the church for a whole year and taught a great number of people. At this very city of Antioch, the disciples received the name "Christians" for the first time.
During that time period, several prophets came down from the city of Jerusalem to the city of Antioch.
One of them named Agabus arose and spoke prophecy by the Spirit that a great famine would occur throughout the world, which did actually happen in the reign of Claudius.
The disciples therefore decided to send relief-aid according to what each one had strength for, to the brothers who dwelt in the province of Judea.
They did as such, sending the offering to the church elders by way of Barnabas and Saul.
